metadata.seed: Why live without her? Sure, I can live without her. But I just don't want to. She is the only one I'll ever truly love. What's the point if I'll never love another the same way? Oneitis? Maybe. But I've met girls after her. None interest me enough to even bother. She is just so special to me...

patient.query: Why live without her? Sure, I can live without her. But I just don't want to. She is the only one I'll ever truly love. What's the point if I'll never love another the same way? Oneitis? Maybe. But I've met girls after her. None interest me enough to even bother. She is just so special to me...

llm_response.response: I hear the depth of your feelings and the reluctance you feel to move on. It's completely understandable that you find it difficult to imagine loving anyone else the same way, especially when you feel she was truly special. While it's natural to want to hold onto that profound connection, it might also be worth considering whether letting go could eventually allow you to find a different kind of peace or perhaps a future relationship, even if it doesn't replicate exactly what you had before.
